name: Coyote
description: Coyote is a prominent figure in many Native American mythologies, often depicted as a trickster, culture hero, and shapeshifter. He is not a deity in the traditional sense of being worshipped with temples and sacrifices, but rather a powerful spirit or being who plays a crucial role in the creation and development of the world. Coyote's origins vary across different tribes and regions, but he is frequently associated with the dawn of time and the emergence of humanity. He is known for his cunning, intelligence, and ability to outsmart others, but also for his impulsiveness, greed, and tendency to make mistakes. These flaws often lead to unintended consequences, sometimes beneficial and sometimes detrimental, shaping the world in unexpected ways. Coyote is a liminal figure, blurring the lines between good and evil, sacred and profane, human and animal. He is a teacher, albeit an unconventional one, imparting lessons through his actions and misadventures. He often introduces death, hardship, and chaos into the world, but also provides essential gifts such as fire, language, and skills. His role is to challenge the status quo, disrupt complacency, and remind people of the complexities and contradictions inherent in life. Coyote's stories serve as cautionary tales, moral lessons, and explanations for the natural world. He is a reminder that even the most powerful beings are fallible, and that change and uncertainty are inevitable aspects of existence. He is revered and respected, but also feared and distrusted, reflecting the ambivalent nature of his character and his influence on the world.
subtitle: Trickster, Creator, Culture Hero, Shapeshifter

keyMyths.title: Coyote Steals Fire
keyMyths.description: Coyote, through cunning and trickery, steals fire from the gods or other beings and brings it to humanity, providing them with warmth and the ability to cook.

keyMyths.title: Coyote Creates the World
keyMyths.description: In some creation myths, Coyote plays a crucial role in shaping the world, either by directly creating landforms or by inadvertently causing changes that lead to the world as it is known.

keyMyths.title: Coyote and the Buffalo
keyMyths.description: Coyote encounters a buffalo and, through his actions, either helps or hinders the buffalo, often resulting in a moral lesson about greed, respect, or the consequences of one's actions.
